events.text: The rest spot they found was a collapsed old civilizational structure, half-buried in sand and rubble, forming a barely windproof space with nearby wall fragments. Small, but enough for four people to squeeze into, enough to distance themselves from the wasteland for a while.
{{characters.elias.name}} sat in the innermost corner of that space.

He didn't pull out his receiver, didn't disassemble anything. Just sat there, back against the metal beam, looking at some place. Nothing was there, just air where his gaze landed but his eyes clearly weren't seeing.
The quality of his silence was different from usual. {{characters.lyra.name}} felt it when she sat down beside him.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: In that Mondris city, when I saw those individuals with completely identical steps, I kept thinking about something.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: That synchronization terrified me not because it was powerful. It was because in that synchronization, every individual disappeared. Not vanished, disappeared.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Those individuals were still there, still moving, still operating. But the thing inside them—that thing that made each person be itself—it was gone.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Disappearance is having something and then not having it. Disappearing is still being there but not being anymore. I think that's more terrifying than actual disappearance.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: You explained that very clearly.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: What were you thinking about in that containment room?

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: I was thinking about you.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: In that underground factory corridor, when we first entered, you said everyone's expression looked like we were going to die, and then you laughed.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: In that white room, I thought about that laugh. I thought, if I was uploaded, that memory would still exist, but it wouldn't be something that made my chest feel something when I remembered it anymore. It would just be data.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Then I thought about {{characters.alea.name}}. At the shield perimeter, she walked into those mechanical soldiers, back to us. I was in a corner interfacing with the system, she was buying me time.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: That feeling of someone supporting you so you can't let that support be wasted. That's not assessment, it's something else. After upload, that thing would be gone.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: And {{characters.soren.name}}. In that corridor saying follow me, just those two words, no explanation, no conditions. I followed him then. I still can't explain why I followed a stranger in that situation.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: But I followed. And then, we got here.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Those things—I don't want an existence without them. No matter how much more powerful that existence might be, no matter how eternally it might continue.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Without those things, that existence wouldn't be me. It would be something else, operating with my memories but not me.
{{characters.lyra.name}} listened through, then didn't speak right away. She let those words settle in her.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: Then you're right.
{{characters.elias.name}} glanced at her, said nothing.
Outside, the wasteland wind kept blowing. The silence in that space shifted into a different texture.
